questions.question: Why did Hari Singh approach Anil?
questions.answer: To gain Anil’s trust and steal from him later.

questions.question: What shows Hari Singh was an experienced thief?
questions.answer: He frequently changed his name to escape police.

questions.question: Why did Anil keep Hari Singh despite bad cooking?
questions.answer: Because Anil was kind and sympathetic.

questions.question: How did Hari Singh earn extra money while shopping?
questions.answer: By cheating and saving about one rupee daily.

questions.question: Why was it hard for Hari Singh to rob Anil?
questions.answer: Because Anil trusted him completely.

questions.question: What does the railway station scene represent?
questions.answer: Hari Singh’s inner moral conflict.

questions.question: Why did Hari Singh value education?
questions.answer: It promised respect and a better future.

questions.question: What quality of Anil reformed Hari Singh?
questions.answer: His compassion and trust.

questions.question: Why did Hari Singh return the stolen money?
questions.answer: Due to guilt and desire to change.

questions.question: What does the wet currency note symbolise?
questions.answer: Anil’s silent forgiveness.

questions.question: What kind of income did Anil have?
questions.answer: Irregular income from writing.

questions.question: Why didn’t Anil lock his money?
questions.answer: Because he was careless and trusting.

questions.question: What fear troubled Hari Singh after the theft?
questions.answer: Fear of losing Anil’s trust.

questions.question: What made Hari Singh hesitate to leave the city?
questions.answer: His conscience and guilt.

questions.question: What change begins in Hari Singh before the theft?
questions.answer: He starts valuing honesty and learning.

questions.question: What role does trust play in the story?
questions.answer: It becomes a force for moral reform.

questions.question: Why does Hari Singh call Anil a careless man?
questions.answer: Because Anil did not protect his money.

questions.question: What does Hari Singh mean by ‘a big man’?
questions.answer: A respected and educated person.

questions.question: What is the main moral conflict in the story?
questions.answer: Choosing between theft and honesty.

questions.question: Why does Anil not confront Hari Singh?
questions.answer: He believes forgiveness can reform him.

questions.question: What does the story suggest about punishment?
questions.answer: Punishment is less effective than kindness.

questions.question: How does first-person narration help the story?
questions.answer: It reveals Hari Singh’s inner thoughts.

questions.question: What lesson does Hari Singh learn at the end?
questions.answer: Honesty brings peace and respect.

questions.question: What social issue is highlighted in the story?
questions.answer: Crime caused by lack of guidance and education.

questions.question: What makes the story realistic?
questions.answer: Its portrayal of human emotions and moral struggle.
